title: Assistance for American Families and Workers
summary: This Treasury Department webpage provides a high-level overview of COVID-19 economic relief programs including Economic Impact Payments, Unemployment Compensation, Child Tax Credit expansions, and Emergency Rental Assistance. These programs were part of the American Rescue Plan and earlier COVID relief legislation from 2020-2021. The page is informational and does not contain current market-moving data, policy decisions, or forward-looking guidance.
key_points: Describes three rounds of Economic Impact Payments disbursed during the COVID-19 crisis
Unemployment Compensation was extended starting March 2021 with some federal taxes on unemployment benefits waived
Child Tax Credit was increased and coverage expanded under the American Rescue Plan
Emergency Rental Assistance program provides funding to government entities to assist households unable to pay rent or utilities
Programs were implemented by Treasury Department, Bureau of the Fiscal Service, and IRS
why_it_matters: This page is not relevant for a finance research archive focused on market structure or macro catalysts.
